title: podman machine start fails for libkrun machines on macOS ARM64: gvproxy exits during ignition reboot, not restarted
body:
## Issue Description

> **Note:** This issue was filed by an AI agent (Claude Sonnet) after an
> extended debugging session on a real system. All version strings, error
> messages, and traces are from actual observed behavior.

`podman machine start` for libkrun machines on macOS ARM64 always fails with:

```
Error: machine did not transition into running state: ssh error: machine is not listening on ssh port
```

The failure occurs when running `podman machine start` directly from the CLI
(not via Podman Desktop). The machine has `LastUp: Never` after multiple days
of attempts with a freshly initialized machine.

## Steps to reproduce the issue

1. `CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=libkrun podman machine init --cpus 1 --memory 38912 --disk-size 250 --rootful my-libkrun`
2. `CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=libkrun podman machine start my-libkrun`

Note: `--cpus 1` is required because 2+ CPUs trigger a separate kernel SMP
lockup in libkrun's GIC emulation (filed against containers/libkrun).

## Describe the results you received

```
Starting machine "my-libkrun"
Error: machine did not transition into running state: ssh error: machine is not listening on ssh port
```

The startup sequence was traced by monitoring process lifetimes and running with
`--log-level debug`:

1. `podman machine start` correctly spawns gvproxy (`-listen-vfkit unixgram://...`) and krunkit.
2. gvproxy immediately begins receiving Ethernet frames from the VM — ARP
   requests from the VM at `192.168.127.1` seeking `192.168.127.2`. Debug output
   shows gvproxy sending **0 bytes back to the VM**.
3. After approximately 30–45 seconds, gvproxy exits with exit code 0.
4. `podman machine start` does not restart gvproxy. It eventually times out and
   returns the error above.

When gvproxy is manually restarted after it exits, it still cannot route SSH:

```
tcpproxy: for incoming conn 127.0.0.1:..., error dialing "192.168.127.2:22":
  connect tcp 192.168.127.2:22: no route to host
```

Two related problems appear to be at play:

**Problem A — gvproxy exits during first-boot ignition reboot:**
CoreOS runs ignition provisioning on first boot and then reboots. When the VM
reboots, its virtio-net connection drops momentarily, causing gvproxy to exit
cleanly. `podman machine start` does not restart gvproxy for this expected
first-boot reboot event, so the VM comes back on its second boot without network
forwarding and SSH never becomes reachable.

**Problem B — IP routing mismatch when gvproxy is restarted:**
gvproxy targets `192.168.127.2` for SSH forwarding, but the VM's IP as seen in
ARP source addresses is `192.168.127.1`. gvproxy has no DHCP state from the
previous session, sends 0 bytes to the VM, and the VM's ARP never resolves.
This suggests the VFKit/libkrun networking mode assigns IPs differently than the
QEMU mode that gvproxy's SSH forwarding target assumes.

## Describe the results you expected

`podman machine start` should handle the ignition-triggered first-boot reboot
for libkrun machines — either by keeping gvproxy alive through the reboot, or
by restarting it after the VM comes back on its second boot.

## podman info output

```yaml
host:
  arch: arm64
  kernel: 6.19.7-200.fc43.aarch64
  os: linux
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    variant: coreos
    version: "43"
version:
  APIVersion: 5.8.2
  BuildOrigin: brew
  GoVersion: go1.26.2
  OsArch: darwin/arm64
  Version: 5.8.2
```

(Full `podman info` from the running applehv machine — libkrun machine could not be started to collect this.)

## Podman in a container

No

## Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged (rootful machine: `--rootful`)

## Upstream Latest Release

Yes — podman 5.8.2 is the latest Homebrew release at time of filing.

## Additional environment details

- MacBook Pro, Apple M4 Pro, 14 cores (10P + 4E), 48 GB RAM
- macOS 26.5 (Build 25F71)
- krunkit 1.1.1 (`slp/homebrew-krunkit` tap), libkrun-efi 1.16.0
- gvproxy v0.8.8 (bundled at `$(brew --prefix podman)/libexec/podman/gvproxy`)
- Guest OS: Fedora CoreOS 43.20260316.3.1 (kernel 6.19.7-200.fc43.aarch64)
- `CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=libkrun` set in environment
- `CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER=applehv` works correctly on the same system

## Additional information

The `--cpus 1` workaround for the libkrun SMP bug (see containers/libkrun) does
not help — the machine still fails to start due to this issue. The combination
of the two bugs means libkrun machines are completely unusable on this system
with current software.

Related: #20122 (Zincati auto-update reboot breaks running VM — similar gvproxy
lifecycle class of issue).

comments.body:
> krunkit 1.1.1 (`slp/homebrew-krunkit` tap), libkrun-efi 1.16.0

This tap is outdated, latest `krunkit` is 1.2.1 and is available from https://github.com/slp/homebrew-krun, can you try to reproduce with the latest version?
For what it’s worth, I was able to successfully start `my-libkrun` with the steps you provided on a M1 mac. `krunkit` was version 1.2.1 (from the podman installer) and podman is 5.8.2
I wasn't able to reproduce this issue either. So, I am closing this.
